scope: TileLang sparse-MLA compressed-dKV P/dP recompute-once gate

input_contract.read: The kernel computes dO.output once per token, recomputes sparse QK probabilities and scaled dP once per token/selected tile, and writes only a tiny checksum sink. It intentionally does not compute dKV. Materialized probs/grad_logits are only used by correctness references when requested.

baseline_context.interpretation: The retained 6.91 ms branch reads materialized P/dP slabs. The all-in-one recompute branch removes those slabs but recomputes full-D attention row terms once per output D block, so it is rejected.

results.read: Best observed S4096 recompute-once sink point.

decision.summary: The recompute-once TileLang kernel is concrete and working, and it cuts the slab-free all-in-one recompute from 89.07 ms to 8.42 ms at S4096. However, 8.42 ms is already the current sparse-atomic dKV wall before doing any dKV accumulation or writeback, so this cannot be a detached precursor stage for the final training backward.
decision.next_kernel_contract: Fuse probability/dP production with the existing selected-limit dKV producer at the point where P/dP values are immediately consumed. Do not write selected-row P/dP slabs, and do not run a separate P/dP recompute sink before dKV. The TileLang branch should either keep the materialized-input selected-limit producer for latency work while integrating P/dP on-chip, or move the fused segmented producer to HIP/CK/rocWMMA if TileLang cannot express the needed reuse and segmented reduction.
